# Setup Instructions: Customer Satisfaction Score Tracking

**Date:** 2026-04-22
**Design:** designs/2026-04-22-customer-satisfaction-score.md
**Generated by:** CSAgent /sf-admin-setup

> Complete these manual Setup steps before or alongside deploying the metadata files from `/sf-object` and `/sf-permission-set`.

**Activities covered:** Page Layout (Case), Custom Report Type

---

## Page Layout: Case — Case Layout

**Setup Path:** Setup → Object Manager → Case → Page Layouts → Case Layout → Edit

### Steps
1. In the palette at the top, search for "Satisfaction Scores" (the related list name)
2. Drag the **Satisfaction Scores** related list into the Related Lists section of the layout
3. Click the wrench icon on the Satisfaction Scores related list to configure columns — add: Score Date (CS_Date__c), Rating (CS_Rating__c), Customer Notes (CS_CustomerNotes__c)
4. Save the layout
5. Assign to profiles: click **Page Layout Assignment** → **Edit Assignment** → assign this layout to Service Agent and Service Manager profiles

### Key Values from Design
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Related List Object | CS_SatisfactionScore__c |
| Related List Label | Satisfaction Scores |
| Related List Columns | CS_Date__c, CS_Rating__c, CS_CustomerNotes__c |

### Notes
- Page layouts for standard objects (Case) must be edited manually — metadata deploy does not reliably update standard object layouts in all orgs
- If multiple Case page layouts exist in the org, repeat for each layout assigned to the relevant profiles
- After adding the related list, test by opening a Case record and verifying the Satisfaction Scores related list appears

---

## Custom Report Type: Cases with Satisfaction Scores

**Setup Path:** Setup → Report Types → New Custom Report Type

### Steps
1. **Primary Object:** Cases
2. **Report Type Label:** Cases with Satisfaction Scores
3. **Report Type Name:** Cases_with_Satisfaction_Scores
4. **Description:** Reports on Cases and their linked CS_SatisfactionScore__c records. Used for CSAT trend analysis by agent and date range.
5. **Deployment Status:** Deployed
6. Click **Next**
7. Click **"Click to relate another object"** → select **Satisfaction Scores** (CS_SatisfactionScore__c)
8. Set relationship: **"Each Cases record may or may not have related Satisfaction Scores records"** (not every Case will have a score)
9. Click **Save**
10. Edit the layout: in the Fields Available for Reports section, drag these CS_ fields into the layout:
    - CS_Rating__c (Rating 1–5)
    - CS_Date__c (Score Date)
    - CS_CustomerNotes__c (Customer Notes)
    - CS_Case__c (Case lookup — for cross-referencing)
11. Save the layout

### Key Values from Design
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Primary Object | Cases |
| Related Object | CS_SatisfactionScore__c (Satisfaction Scores) |
| Relationship Type | May or may not have (0..1) |
| Key Report Fields | CS_Rating__c, CS_Date__c, CS_CustomerNotes__c |

### Notes
- Users need "Create and Customize Reports" permission to build reports from this type — ensure Service Manager profile has this
- Test by creating a sample report: Reports → New Report → select "Cases with Satisfaction Scores" type → add CS_Rating__c and Case Owner Name columns → group by Case Owner → verify data appears
- After creating the report, build the CSAT trend dashboard: Dashboards → New Dashboard → add a Line Chart component using this report, group by CS_Date__c (month), Y-axis = Average of CS_Rating__c

---

## What's Handled by Metadata Deploy (not in this file)

The following are deployed via `sf project deploy start` — no manual Setup needed:
- `CS_SatisfactionScore__c` object and all 4 fields → `/sf-object` ✅
- `CS_Rating_Range_1_to_5` validation rule → `/sf-object` ✅
- `CS_ServiceAgentPermissions` permission set → `/sf-permission-set` ✅

## What Still Requires Manual Setup (not covered by any command)

- **Flow:** `CS_CreateSatisfactionScoreOnCaseClosure` — build manually in Flow Builder or use `/sf-flow` to generate the metadata XML
- **OWD:** Set CS_SatisfactionScore__c to Public Read/Write in Setup → Sharing Settings
- **Dashboard:** Build after creating the Custom Report Type and sample report above
